cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. So first, I need to check the timeline of his day based on the witnesses' accounts. Andrew woke up at 5am. Then William saw him playing tennis from 5am to 6am. After that, Jessica saw him buying lunch at the deli from 6am to 9am. Then Tiffany saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 9am to 11am. John saw Andrew waiting at the train station from 11am to 5pm. Sarah saw him reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. Also, the clothing store was closed after 9pm. So the key here is to figure out what times Andrew was not accounted for. The clothing store is open until 9pm, so he could have gone there only during the times he wasn't seen elsewhere. Let's break down each time period: - 5am to 6am: playing tennis with William. - 6am to 9am: buying lunch with Jessica. - 9am to 11am: taking photos with Tiffany. - 11am to 5pm: waiting at the train station with John. - 6pm to 9pm: reading at the library with Sarah. Wait, so between 5am and 6pm, Andrew is accounted for. Let me check the time gaps: From 5am to 6am: occupied. From 6am to 9am: occupied. From 9am to 11am: occupied. From 11am to 5pm: occupied. Then from 5pm to 6pm: is there any activity? The next event is Sarah seeing him at 6pm. So between 5pm and 6pm, there's a gap. After that, from 6pm to 9pm, he's at the library. The clothing store is closed after 9pm, so he must have gone before then. So the only time when Andrew is not seen by anyone is between 5pm and 6pm.
